senses.definition: a small restaurant that sells drinks and meals

examples.x: The cozy cafe on the corner served delicious pastries and freshly brewed coffee.
Students often gathered at the nearby cafe to study and socialize over cups of tea.
The quaint cafe offered a relaxing ambiance with soft music playing in the background.
We met at the neighborhood cafe for brunch and caught up on each other's lives.
The French-style cafe boasted an extensive menu of gourmet sandwiches and desserts.

senses.definition: a place in which works of art are shown or sold to the public

examples.x: She spent the afternoon wandering through the gallery, admiring the vibrant paintings and thought-provoking sculptures.
The gallery's latest installation focuses on contemporary issues, using art as a medium for social commentary.
Many people attended the opening night at the gallery, eager to meet the artists and discuss their work.
The gallery offers workshops for aspiring artists to learn new techniques and improve their skills.

senses.definition: a place that is open during nighttime in which people can dance, eat, and drink

examples.x: We decided to go to the nightclub downtown to celebrate Jane's birthday.
The nightclub was packed with people dancing to the DJ's music.
I love the atmosphere of the nightclub with its colorful lights and upbeat music.
The nightclub has a strict dress code, so make sure to dress appropriately.
The nightclub is known for hosting famous DJs and live music events.

senses.definition: a building where firefighters stay and have the tools they need to help with fires and other emergencies

examples.x: The fire station dispatched several units to extinguish the blaze at the downtown apartment building.
Children from the local school visited the fire station to learn about fire safety and meet the firefighters.
The fire station's alarm sounded, prompting firefighters to quickly gear up and rush to the scene of the car accident.
Residents gathered at the fire station for a community meeting to discuss emergency preparedness and evacuation procedures.

senses.definition: a place with special equipment that people go to exercise or play sports

examples.x: He goes to the gym five times a week.
The gym was crowded with people after work.
She joined a new gym near her house.
They offer yoga classes at the gym.
I saw her lifting weights at the gym yesterday.

senses.definition: a playing area built outdoors for children, particularly inside parks or schools

examples.x: The children spent the afternoon playing at the playground.
The school built a new playground with a climbing wall.
Parents watched their kids run around the playground.
The playground includes swings, slides, and a sandbox.
Safety mats were installed under the equipment in the playground.

senses.definition: a shop where a barber works and men can get haircuts
addressing the structures, functions, or issues of cities and their populations

examples.x: Urban studies examine the challenges of housing in densely populated areas.
The government initiated an urban development project to address infrastructure issues.
Urban sociology explores how people interact within city environments.
Researchers studied urban migration patterns over the last decade.
Urban policy reforms aim to reduce traffic congestion in major cities.

senses.definition: a residential area outside a city

examples.x: After years of living in the city, they decided to move to a suburb to enjoy a quieter lifestyle and more space for their growing family.
The suburb is known for its excellent schools and family-friendly parks, making it a popular choice for young parents.
Many commuters prefer living in the suburb, as it allows them to escape the hustle and bustle of city life while still being close enough to work.
The new shopping mall on the outskirts of the suburb has become a central hub for residents, offering a variety of dining and entertainment options.
In the suburb, neighbors often gather for community events, fostering a strong sense of camaraderie and support among residents.

senses.definition: the outer areas or parts of a city or town

examples.x: Many families choose to live on the outskirts of the city to enjoy a quieter lifestyle while still having access to urban amenities.
The new shopping center was built on the outskirts, making it a convenient destination for residents from nearby neighborhoods.
As the city expanded, the outskirts became increasingly populated, leading to the development of new schools and parks to accommodate the growing community.
The outskirts of the town are known for their beautiful landscapes, with rolling hills and open fields that attract nature lovers and outdoor enthusiasts.
Commuting from the outskirts to the city center can be challenging during rush hour, as traffic congestion often slows down travel times significantly.

senses.definition: a town or city's upper area
an area close to the center of a city that usually suffers from economic problems

examples.x: Community leaders are working to revitalize the inner city by investing in affordable housing and economic development initiatives.
Many residents of the inner city rely on public transportation to commute to work or access essential services.
Urban gardens and green spaces are helping to beautify the inner city and provide residents with access to fresh produce.
Nonprofit organizations in the inner city offer social services and support programs to help address poverty and homelessness.
The inner city is home to a diverse population, including immigrants, working-class families, and young professionals, contributing to its vibrant cultural scene.

senses.definition: a group of people who live in the same area

examples.x: The local community came together to organize a charity fundraiser.
She volunteered her time to help improve the community park.
Our community holds regular meetings to discuss neighborhood issues.
The school is an important part of the community, providing education and resources.
They moved to a new city and quickly became involved in their new community.

senses.definition: a person who regularly travels to city for work

examples.x: The train is always packed with commuters during rush hour.
She’s a daily commuter who spends two hours traveling to work.
Many commuters prefer public transport to avoid traffic.
A new app helps commuters find the fastest route to their destinations.
The train station was crowded with commuters heading to the city.

senses.definition: the number of people who live in a particular city or country

examples.x: As the population ages, there will be increasing strain on healthcare systems.
Cities struggle to build enough housing to accommodate rising populations.
Migration patterns have changed the ethnic composition of population groups over decades.
The population of New York City exceeds eight million people.
Japan has a rapidly aging population, leading to economic challenges.

senses.definition: buildings in which people live, including their condition, prices, or types

examples.x: Housing prices have increased this year.
Many families struggle to find suitable housing.
The new policy aims to improve rural housing.
Good housing conditions improve people’s quality of life.

senses.definition: an area in which people leave their vehicles

examples.x: I had to park in the back of the parking lot because all the spots near the entrance were taken.
They just finished repaving the parking lot, and it looks much better now.
I forgot where I parked, so I had to walk around the parking lot until I found my car.
The parking lot outside the grocery store is always crowded on weekends.
We found a spot in the parking lot right next to the entrance, which was super convenient.

senses.definition: a sign that shows warnings or information to drivers

examples.x: The road sign warned drivers of a sharp curve ahead.
He slowed down after seeing the road sign for a pedestrian crossing.
The road sign indicated a speed limit of 50 kilometers per hour.
She missed her turn because the road sign was obscured by a tree.
The road sign showed the distance to the next gas station.

senses.definition: a person who is on foot and not in or on a vehicle

examples.x: The bridge offers a separate pedestrian lane for those who wish to walk or jog.
Pedestrians should always use the sidewalk and avoid walking on the road.
As a pedestrian, you must be cautious when crossing busy roads.
The pedestrian crossed the street at the designated crosswalk.

senses.definition: a tall post with a light on top, usually found along roads, streets, or sidewalks

examples.x: The street lights flickered on as the sun dipped below the horizon, casting a warm glow over the city streets.
The driver slowed down as they approached the intersection, guided by the amber glow of the street lights overhead.
Residents petitioned the city council to install additional street lights in their neighborhood to improve safety and security after dark.
The city's sustainability initiative aims to reduce energy consumption by replacing traditional street lights with energy-efficient LED alternatives.

senses.definition: a narrow path in the countryside

examples.x: They strolled down the quiet lane, enjoying the peaceful countryside views.
The lane was lined with blooming wildflowers on both sides.
He took the scenic lane through the village to avoid the main road.
The old farmhouse was located at the end of a long, winding lane.
The lane was perfect for a leisurely bike ride on a sunny day.

senses.definition: a type of bridge that is built over a road to provide a different passage
the place where a road is crossed by another

examples.x: The small village is located at the crossroad of two major highways.
The traffic lights at the crossroad weren’t working, causing confusion.
The car stopped at the crossroad to check for oncoming traffic.
The police officer directed traffic at the bustling crossroad.
The crossroad was a common meeting point for travelers in ancient times.

senses.definition: a land that is used for farming, especially in rural areas
a large, open, and grass-covered area

examples.x: Many animals live in the vast grassland.
The grassland stretches for miles without trees.
Fires can spread quickly in dry grasslands.
Farmers use grasslands for cattle grazing.
The grassland is home to antelopes and zebras.

senses.definition: (in the US) one of the areas into which a state is divided and has a local government of its own

examples.x: The county fair is an annual event that draws people from all over the region to celebrate agriculture and community.
Our county government provides essential services such as law enforcement, public education, and road maintenance.
The historic courthouse in the center of town serves as the administrative hub for the county.
Local farmers in the county grow a variety of crops, including corn, soybeans, and wheat, contributing to the region's agricultural economy.

senses.definition: an area of land that is usually enclosed and is used to grow fruit trees in
a deep hole dug in the ground to get access to the resources beneath, such as water, oil, etc.
a restaurant where mainly pizza is served
a huge wall built to keep water from entering an area or to contain and use it as a power source to produce electricity

examples.x: The dam controls the river’s water level.
Engineers designed the dam to generate hydroelectric power.
The town relies on the dam for its water supply.
The construction of the dam displaced several villages.
Heavy rains put pressure on the dam’s structure.

senses.definition: a pathway typically made of concrete or asphalt at the side of a street for people to walk on

examples.x: She walked along the sidewalk on her way to the bus stop.
The city plans to repave the sidewalk near the park.
The dog pulled its owner off the sidewalk and onto the grass.
He found a quiet spot to sit on the sidewalk and enjoy his coffee.
The sidewalk was crowded with pedestrians during rush hour.

senses.definition: the place where someone or a group of people come from and feel a strong connection to
an open area in a city or town where two or more streets meet

examples.x: The market was held in the town square.
They met in the square to watch the street performers.
Tourists gathered in the square to take photos.
He sat on a bench in the square and read a book.
Children played in the fountain at the center of the square.
